seoTitle: How to Share Documents in a Microsoft Teams Meeting (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to upload and co-edit files live during a Microsoft Teams meeting using the in-call chat panel and OneDrive integration.
intro: Getting everyone on the same page during a live call works best when you can share and even co-edit a document without pausing to send a clunky email. This guide covers opening the in-meeting chat panel, uploading a file from OneDrive or your device, setting view or edit permissions for attendees, and using real-time co-authoring. It's built for anyone running client calls or team reviews inside Teams.

steps.heading: Open the meeting chat panel
steps.body: During your live call, open the in-meeting Chat side panel from the control bar.

steps.heading: Attach your file
steps.body: Click the Actions and apps plus icon, select Attach file, and choose between OneDrive or uploading directly from your device.

steps.heading: Set sharing permissions
steps.body: Use the link permissions dropdown to configure view, comment, or edit access, and switch to Specific people if you need to authorize external guests.

steps.heading: Send and open the file
steps.body: Add an optional message and send it to the meeting, then open the shared file inside Teams to enable real-time co-authoring.

steps.heading: Review after the meeting
steps.body: Check the persistent Shared tab after the call ends to find every document that was sent without digging through the chat history.

faq.question: Can multiple people edit a shared document at the same time in a Teams meeting?
faq.answer: Yes, once opened inside Teams, files like Word or Excel documents support real-time co-authoring with multiple attendees typing simultaneously.

faq.question: How do I let an external guest access a file I share?
faq.answer: Set the sharing permissions to Specific people to securely authorize access for guests outside your organization.

faq.question: Where do shared files go after the meeting ends?
faq.answer: You can find them anytime in the persistent Shared tab, which keeps a permanent record without needing to scroll through chat logs.

faq.question: Can I share a file from OneDrive without downloading it first?
faq.answer: Yes, the Actions and apps menu lets you attach cloud-based OneDrive files directly without needing a local copy.
